summary: Restored repository subtree handling after earlier squashed PRs (#463 and #719) caused Git to stop recognizing affected paths as subtrees. The PR explicitly warned not to squash the fix and also updated the vendored asm-differ tool to a newer upstream snapshot, including dependency updates and several asm-differ feature/refactor changes.
what_changed: Fixed Git subtree state for vendored tool paths; PR body says #463 and #719 were squashed, so Git did not believe those paths were subtrees and could not merge them or use other git subtree functionality.
Updated tools/asm-differ to a newer commit, touching diff.py heavily and adding a Poetry pyproject.toml.

Updated Python dependency pins in requirements.txt: colorama 0.4.5 to 0.4.6, coverage 6.4.4 to 7.1.0, python_Levenshtein 0.12.2 to 0.20.9, watchdog 2.1.9 to 2.2.1, and added GitPython 3.1.30; capstone was removed from the visible requirements excerpt.
Updated asm-differ dependency docs from python-Levenshtein to levenshtein and cxxfilt.
Added asm-differ .gitignore entries for .vscode/ and poetry.lock.
Added DiffMode enum in asm-differ and replaced the old threeway string option with explicit modes: single, single_base, normal, 3prev, and 3base.
Added asm-differ CLI options for single current view (-1), single base view (-0), and disabling .rodata reference display (-R/--no-show-rodata-refs).
Refactored asm-differ table formatting around TableLine and TableData rather than passing table metadata plus tuples of OutputLine objects.
Added project setting expected_dir with default expected/ and used it when locating reference object files.
Changed map-file search to distinguish object lookup from binary address lookup via a for_binary flag.

Enhanced asm-differ architecture handling with mipsee settings, shared MIPS_ARCH_NAMES, .late_rodata reference support, R_MIPS_GPREL32 support, R_ARM_V4BX handling, and per-architecture is_end_of_function hooks.
Changed Levenshtein diff fallback logic so oversized or unsupported remapping falls back to difflib instead of relying on earlier hard size checks.
tools/m2ctx/README.md appears to have only a trailing newline/no-newline-at-EOF style change in the provided diff.
smart_moves: Used an unsquashed PR to repair subtree ancestry; the PR body explicitly says not to squash because squashing is what broke Git subtree recognition.
Marked the PR title with [skip editorconfig], likely to avoid automated formatting churn on imported subtree content; evidence is limited to the title.
Updated vendored asm-differ as a subtree-style import rather than attempting to hand-minimize the diff, preserving compatibility with future upstream subtree merges.
Introduced DiffMode enum to avoid fragile stringly-typed threeway/single-mode logic.
Separated table data from formatter output with TableData/TableLine, making plain, ANSI, HTML, and JSON formatters consume one uniform representation.
Made single-view modes avoid requiring or reading base/reference object files when not needed.
Made .rodata reference rendering configurable, which helps when jump-table/data-reference noise obscures a decompilation comparison.
Added expected_dir configuration so projects with nonstandard expected object layouts can reuse asm-differ without hardcoded expected/ assumptions.
Factored end-of-function detection into architecture processors, making stop-at-return behavior extensible beyond hardcoded MIPS/PPC/i686 checks.
decomp_lessons: Do not squash PRs that are intended to preserve or repair git subtree history; subtree operations depend on recognizable merge ancestry.
When importing or updating vendored tools, preserve upstream layout and metadata where possible so future subtree pulls/merges remain practical.
Use explicit enums for tool modes that drive many downstream branches; it prevents accidental confusion between threeway, normal diff, and single-view behavior.
Avoid hardcoded project paths in decomp tools; settings such as expected_dir make the same tooling usable across repositories with different build layouts.
For diff tools, separate comparison data from rendering so terminal, HTML, JSON, and plain text outputs stay consistent.
Architecture-specific assembly behavior belongs in architecture processors, especially return detection, relocation handling, and symbol/branch normalization.
Matching workflows benefit from optional display controls, such as hiding .rodata-to-.text references when they create visual noise.
naming_conventions: DiffMode enum members use uppercase names with lowercase wire/string values: SINGLE='single', SINGLE_BASE='single_base', NORMAL='normal', THREEWAY_PREV='3prev', THREEWAY_BASE='3base'.
New asm-differ data containers use descriptive names TableLine and TableData rather than TableMetadata plus tuple-of-lines conventions.
Project setting expected_dir follows existing snake_case settings style and defaults to 'expected/'.
Shared MIPS architecture detection was named MIPS_ARCH_NAMES and includes mips, mipsel, and mipsee.
CLI option naming keeps negative display toggles explicit: --no-show-rodata-refs stores show_rodata_refs=false.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added single-base and single-current modes so asm-differ can act as an assembly viewer rather than always forcing a target/current diff.
Added optional suppression of serialized .rodata references before objdump output; useful when jump table references are not relevant to the immediate match.
Extended ELF rodata reference parsing to include .late_rodata and MIPS relocation type R_MIPS_GPREL32, in addition to R_MIPS_32.
Added MIPSEE architecture settings using objdump arch flag mips:5900.
Handled MIPS jumps to .text symbols specially so text-relative j instructions can be treated as branch targets.
Added R_ARM_V4BX ignore handling because that relocation can rewrite bx register instructions to mov pc,register on some targets.
Improved stop-at-return through processor.is_end_of_function: MIPS waits until after jr ra delay-slot behavior, PPC checks blr, and base implementation defaults false.
Changed Levenshtein sequence diff remapping to catch ValueError and fall back to difflib when too many unique elements make chr-based remapping invalid.
Expanded parenthesis splitting guard from only %hi/%lo to also avoid splitting %got, %call16, and %gp_rel macro operands.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
One review by r-burns approved the PR with an empty body.
The PR body itself gave operational review guidance: 'Needless to say, do not squash this PR.'
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that updated tools/asm-differ via git subtree after PR #727.
Search for usage of expected_dir in project settings or local asm-differ configs.
Search for later asm-differ changes involving DiffMode, single mode, or rodata reference display.
Check whether GitPython added in requirements.txt was used by subtree/tooling workflows elsewhere.
